description: A designed DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card in the series template: deep navy-blue background with a faint large American-flag watermark and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular black-and-white candid family photo of four smiling people (an older woman, a younger woman, and a man in glasses behind, with another woman in front) embracing together outdoors at a gathering. Below a large white quotation mark, the white testimonial reads: "I think of all the sacrifices my parents made just for the hope of having a different life. Just a dream and idea. They did not know how things were going to work out for our family. I think of how incredibly brave and resilient my parents were to leap into this life with that hope." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Myung K. / TSA, Acting Chief of Staff / 2nd generation from South Korea.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right. This is pro-immigrant-heritage workforce-spotlight messaging from the prior administration emphasizing family sacrifice and the American dream; target audience is the public and DHS workforce.
notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "I think of all the sacrifices my parents made just for the hope of having a different life...how incredibly brave and resilient my parents were to leap into this life with that hope."; Myung K., TSA, Acting Chief of Staff, 2nd generation from South Korea
